$comment: c-TF (D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-DECAY-IN-DEREF, fn-pointer-ARRAY facet): `*(tbl)` where tbl is an ARRAY of function pointers decays to Ptr<Ptr<FnSig>> (C 6.3.2.1p3), so `(*(tbl))(x)` == `tbl[0](x)`. BOTH tiers agree the deref is the fn-ptr: cst_to_hir combineUnaryOp decays the array (the FnSig fold reads the DECAYED Ptr and does NOT collapse), and the semantic-tier subtreeType Deref MIRRORS the decay so checkCall resolves the callee. `tbl[i](x)` (Index) already worked (fnptr_array_inferred); THIS is the DIRECT-deref call form. RED-ON-DISABLE (each independently → no compile): revert cst_to_hir Deref decay → `*(tbl)` TYPELESS → H0001; revert semantic-tier Deref decay → callee InvalidType → S0004 S_NotCallable. VALUE-CORRECT + RUNTIME-DRIVEN: tbl[0] is assigned at runtime through the opaque pick() index so (*(tbl)) is not const-folded; tbl[0]=add40 → (*(tbl))(2)=add40(2)=42. Pure c-subset → all 4 targets; pe RUNS on Windows, elf/arm64 RUN on linux/qemu.
